patches.find: Note on sources: Reid's acoustic measurements of the King's Chamber sarcophagus are documented in his cymatics research and conference presentations, not in peer-reviewed acoustics journals. The dome model cites 'Reid 1997' without a verifiable academic reference. The underlying acoustic measurement is real; the attribution to dome cosmology is unfounded.
patches.replace:
Note on sources: Reid's acoustic measurements of the King's Chamber sarcophagus are documented in his cymatics research and conference presentations, not in peer-reviewed acoustics journals. The dome model cites 'Reid 1997' without a verifiable academic reference. The underlying acoustic measurement is real; the attribution to dome cosmology is unfounded.

On the harmonic claim: The dome's WIN list describes 117 Hz as 'consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' but never specifies which base frequency is intended. If the base is 7.83 Hz (Schumann, WIN-002), then 10 × 7.83 = 78.3 Hz ≠ 117 Hz — off by a factor of 1.49. If the base is 11.7 Hz (Tesla, WIN-001), then 10 × 11.7 = 117 Hz — a numerical coincidence with no physical mechanism connecting electromagnetic dome resonances to acoustic vibrations in stone. No prediction was registered before Reid's measurements; this is a post-hoc pattern match.
patches.rationale: ISS-311: Address the dome's vague 'consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' claim directly. Show it is either numerically wrong (Schumann: 78.3 Hz ≠ 117 Hz) or mechanistically empty (Tesla: numerical coincidence with no EM-to-acoustic coupling). Establishes that the claim is post-hoc, not prospective.

patches.find: The dome model has no equation linking eclipses to magnetic perturbations — it simply observes that the signal exists and claims it as a 'WIN' without deriving the magnitude or geometry from dome parameters.
patches.replace: The dome model has no equation linking eclipses to magnetic perturbations — it simply observes that the signal exists and claims it as a 'WIN' without deriving the magnitude or geometry from dome parameters. Note: the dome model's own eclipse validation in monitor.py imposes a Kp < 2 quiet-day filter — an escape clause that voids the test whenever geomagnetic activity is elevated. This means the 'confirmation' is conditionally testable only under ideal conditions chosen by the dome itself, which further undermines the claim's robustness.
patches.rationale: ISS-312: Add the Kp < 2 escape clause from monitor.py, which is both accurate (per curmudgeon code analysis) and strengthens the critique by showing the dome's own software treats the confirmation as conditional rather than robust.

patches.find: Published only in non-indexed journal. Replication attempts explain results via classical near-field coupling. CUORE experiment shows expected Faraday attenuation.
patches.replace: Published only in non-indexed journals with no peer-reviewed replications. Meyl's scalar wave framework has been mathematically refuted: Bruhn (2006, TU Darmstadt) showed that Meyl misapplies transverse wave equations as longitudinal, uses an incorrect functional form (E=E(r(t)) rather than E=E(r,t)), and produces equations that violate the superposition principle — there are no solutions of the Meyl type within Maxwell's electrodynamics. Replication experiments are explained by classical near-field (reactive) coupling: at close range, capacitive and inductive coupling through apertures and seams in imperfect Faraday enclosures produces apparent shielding failures with no new physics required. If scalar waves genuinely bypassed Faraday shielding, every precision physics experiment worldwide — from LIGO to KATRIN — would show unexplained low-frequency interference. They do not.
patches.rationale: ISS-023: Replace the tangential and broken CUORE citation (cuore.lngs.infn.it returns HTTP 404 per 2026-04-06 integrity check) with Bruhn's rigorous mathematical refutation of Meyl's theoretical framework, which is the correct response to a theoretical-physics-level claim. Add the near-field reactive coupling explanation.

patches.find: No peer-reviewed replication; CUORE shows standard shielding.
patches.replace: No peer-reviewed replication; Meyl's scalar wave framework is mathematically refuted (Bruhn, 2006, TU Darmstadt). Classical near-field reactive coupling explains all reported results without invoking new physics.
patches.rationale: ISS-023: Remove broken CUORE link (HTTP 404) and align verdict text with the stronger mathematical refutation argument in the updated evidence field.

patches.find: the acceleration is real and scientifically interesting, but fully explained by standard core dynamics (competing flux lobes; Livermore et al., 2020).
patches.replace: the acceleration is real and scientifically interesting — peer-reviewed records show the NMP drifting at approximately 15 km/yr in 1989 and accelerating to ~60 km/yr by 2002, a fourfold increase concentrated in roughly 13 years (not a gradual trend spanning five decades). This is fully explained by standard core dynamics: Livermore et al. (2020) trace the acceleration to elongation of the Canadian negative flux lobe on the core-mantle boundary between 1970–1999 (competing flux lobes).
patches.rationale: ISS-090: Specify that the NMP acceleration was concentrated in the 1990s (fourfold over ~13 years, per Chulliat & Newitt 2010 and Livermore 2020). This corrects any implication of a gradual trend and makes our description more accurate than the dome's own framing, improving credibility.

patches.find: standard geodetic calculations using spherical geometry (Vincenty, 1975) achieve sub-0.5% error (R² > 0.995) when properly accounting for ellipsoid shape and curvature.
patches.replace: standard geodetic calculations on the WGS84 ellipsoid achieve sub-millimeter absolute accuracy — effectively zero systematic error at any route scale (Vincenty, 1975; Karney, 2013). The globe model requires no latitude-dependent correction factor because its geometry is correct from first principles; the dome's quadratic correction is an admission of geometric failure, not a confirmed prediction.
patches.rationale: ISS-019: Replace imprecise "sub-0.5% error" (Vincenty achieves ~0.5 mm, not 0.5% of route length) with the accurate "sub-millimeter accuracy". Add Karney 2013 which improves further to sub-nanometer. Key insight: globe needs zero correction factors; dome needs a latitude-dependent fudge factor, which is itself evidence of failure.

patches.find: This is a linguistic/theological claim based on Hebrew word etymology, not a physical discovery. Both cited verses use figurative language — Job 37:18 compares the sky to a mirror via simile, while Deuteronomy 28:23 describes drought curses.
patches.replace: This is a linguistic/theological claim based on Hebrew word etymology, not a physical discovery. Both cited verses use figurative language — Job 37:18 compares the sky to a mirror via explicit simile, while Deuteronomy 28:23 describes drought curses. Mainstream biblical scholarship reads both as rhetorical: Job 37:18's 'strong as a cast metal mirror' is a simile describing the sky's apparent hardness on a clear day, and Deuteronomy 28:23's 'copper heavens' is a drought curse — a rhetorical image attested across ancient Near Eastern literature with no cosmological intent. Neither verse is interpreted as a physical specification of a metallic dome in any academic biblical commentary tradition. The dome model treats poetic imagery as a cosmological engineering specification.
patches.rationale: ISS-322: Add that mainstream biblical scholarship reads both verses as metaphorical/rhetorical. This is the appropriate response to a scriptural claim: show that even within the text's own interpretive tradition, it does not support a literal metallic dome.

patches.find: Dome model lacks gravitational lensing mechanism; citation alone does not substitute.
patches.replace: Dome model has no mathematical derivation of the RAR, no equation predicting the critical acceleration scale from dome parameters, and no spatial framework for galaxy-scale objects (dome firmament peaks at 9,086 km; observed galaxies lie at megaparsec distances). MOND (Milgrom, 1983) already provides a rigorous framework for the RAR with 40 years of prior art; the dome offers no improvement over MOND and none of MOND's testable predictions.
patches.rationale: ISS-053: The dome's argument is about the RAR's empirical tightness supporting an aetheric medium, not about having a lensing theory. Replace the misleading "no lensing mechanism" framing with the correct critique: no RAR derivation, no critical acceleration equation, no galaxy-scale ontology. Add MOND as the relevant prior framework the dome must surpass.

patches.find: See also WIN-047, which claims the same distance-redshift relationship using different standard candles — a salami-slicing duplication (Section 6.1, Pattern #4).
patches.replace: Four independent distance methods — Cepheids, SBF, TRGB (tip of the red giant branch), and Type Ia supernovae — agree to within a few percent across overlapping distance ranges (Anand et al., 2022), making a systematic error conspiracy across four independent measurement techniques implausible. See also WIN-047, which claims the same distance-redshift relationship using different standard candles — a salami-slicing duplication (Section 6.1, Pattern #4).
patches.rationale: ISS-363: The original Pantheon+ DOI issue is already resolved in the current text (SH0ES/Cepheid citation in place). This patch strengthens the argument by adding the cross-validation statement: four independent methods agree across overlapping ranges, making a systematic error conspiracy across all four implausible.

patches.find: The dome model states that gravity arises from the aetheric pressure gradient, which depends on the column height H(r). If gravity scales with H(r) — the model's own stated mechanism for gravitational effects — then g_rim/g_pole = 837/8,537 = 0.098, a 90% drop in gravity at the Antarctic rim. Even if the dome claims a nonlinear relationship, any monotonic function of H(r) still predicts dramatically stronger gravity at the pole (H=8,537 km) than at the rim (H=837 km), far exceeding the observed 0.53% variation.
patches.replace: The dome model's stated physics links gravity to the aetheric pressure gradient driven by column height H(r). Under any mechanism where gravitational effects depend monotonically on H(r) — linearly, as a power law, or otherwise — the 10× difference in H(r) between pole and rim must translate to substantially different gravity. Under linear scaling: g_rim/g_pole = 837/8,537 = 0.098, a 90% drop. Under square-root scaling: √(837/8,537) ≈ 0.31, a 69% drop. To reconcile any such coupling with the observed 0.53% variation, the function relating H(r) to gravity would need to be essentially flat over a 10× variation in H(r) — at which point H(r) is irrelevant to gravity and the stated mechanism is vacuous. If the dome instead claims gravity is independent of H(r), it must specify an alternative mechanism consistent with the observed 0.53% variation; no such mechanism appears in V51.0.
patches.rationale: ISS-411: Address the potential objection that H(r) does not explicitly govern gravity. Show that ANY monotonic coupling (linear, square-root, etc.) predicts catastrophic gravity variation inconsistent with observation. The only escape is to claim H(r) is irrelevant — which eliminates the stated mechanism. This makes the self-contradiction robust against the objection.

patches.find: The dome model has no MHD equations, no core dynamics, and no mechanism for generating reversed-flux patches. It cannot explain why the SAA splits, only that it does. Note: V51.0 now acknowledges WIN-004's 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.'
patches.replace: The dome model has no MHD equations, no core dynamics, and no mechanism for generating reversed-flux patches. It cannot explain why the SAA splits, only that it does. A stronger discriminating test: the two SAA cells drift westward together at ~0.2–0.3°/year while slowly separating at ~0.8°/year — the signature of flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell, not aetheric vortex decay. MHD also explains the observed asymmetry: the African cell decays faster than the American cell despite both lying at similar distances from the pole, because the African large-low-shear-velocity province alters local core flow. The dome's radially symmetric aetheric vortex has no mechanism for azimuthal asymmetry. Note: V51.0 now acknowledges WIN-004's 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.'
patches.rationale: ISS-013: Add the westward drift and asymmetric decay rate arguments (from curmudgeon review), which are more specific and harder to dismiss than the general MHD claim. The asymmetric decay between African and American cells specifically falsifies the dome's axisymmetric aetheric vortex prediction.
